title: Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast demo

The '''''Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'' demo''' was a pre-release demo for ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''. It consisted of a single, unique level: an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] base the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyle Katarn]] must infiltrate.

==Plot summary==
This demo contained an original level, where [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jan Ors]] investigate an [[Imperial Remnant]] base on [[Alzoc III/Legends|Alzoc III]], the [[Talz/Legends|Talz]] home planet. Kyle is dropped off hidden in a cargo crate by Jan flying the ''[[Raven's Claw]]''. He then enters the base, fighting off [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]]. Meanwhile, Jan gets surrounded by Imperials and Kyle has to rush to rescue her. En route he confronts a [[Reborn]] that tries to stop him, to no avail.

==Continuity==
*The demo is unlockable in the Xbox and GameCube versions of ''Jedi Knight II'' by simply entering the word "DEMO" in the cheat menu.
*It is uncertain when this demo is set. The opening scroll places it [[12 ABY/Legends|8 years after]] the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. This scroll is identical to that of [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast|''Jedi Outcast'']] except for the final paragraph. As ''Outcast'' has since been placed elsewhere, it is likely this is also not the correct setting for this mission. Currently the [[Mission at Alzoc III (Empire Reborn)|mission]] is placed in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] on this wiki as a bridge between the two arcs of the [[Empire Reborn]] campaign.
*The sound files used in this level, including scripted dialog, are all lifted from various points in the full game.
*When overlooking the [[Reborn]] "boss" from outside the hangar, looking through the window with your binoculars will cause him to turn and give a demented smile at you, however, this is a graphics bug encountered on some graphics cards rather than an intended feature.
*The demo uses an original level to showcase [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]/[[The Force/Legends|Force]] powers, as in the early levels of ''Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'', Kyle does not use a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]. Some claimed ''Dark Forces II'' was "just like every other FPS, until you [get] your lightsaber." It is likely that a ''Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast'' demo that did not showcase the game's primary selling point would effect negatively on sales and was hence, the lightsaber was included as the primary weapon for the demo.
